mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion and rising consumer purchasing power, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 139118k as of 2014-08-01 shows sustained employment growth
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS momentum signal falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during periods of PAYEMS growth > 150k are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth, real rates turn negative, incentivizing equity allocation over cash and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 237.909 as of 2014-08-01
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with positive inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during negative real rate regimes do not exceed baseline returns by at least 2% annualized, or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter acceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate increases relative to prior quarter
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 acceleration signal falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns following GDP acceleration are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premium widens.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following UNRATE spikes > 0.3pp are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints, which compress profit margins and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines > 0.5% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.15), or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer demand and residential investment, which together reduce aggregate demand and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings forecasts are cut.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month change < -0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month change < -5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 444377M and HOUST at 1093k as of 2014-08-01
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during joint RSAFS and HOUST contractions are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2%, the central bank faces policy lag risk and market expectations of future rate hikes compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.909 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2014-08-01
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during high-inflation, low-rate regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.2 million units annualized signals robust residential investment and forward-looking consumer confidence, which drives construction employment and materials demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average > 1.2 million units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1093k as of 2014-07-01 shows recovery trajectory
regime_hint: post-housing-crisis recovery with normalization of residential investment
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of HOUST > 1.2M are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
